Way in Day Step 3, Food Sale and Final Pick. So this is the last part of the nutrition and way in day. This happens with every single play you ever do. This is the process. These are the logistics. You're going to follow this thing end to end. Alright, so it says food sale and final pick and what do I mean by that? So once you have completed the supplement sale, you're going to transition to the food sale. So you are now done. They've signed everything and you say, well were you planning on buying and prepping all this for yourself? Would you prefer some help? And they're going to be like, well I prefer some help, I guess. And you're like, awesome. What meal do you struggle the most with? Most people are going to say lunch because that's the one that they eat out with or they'll say breakfast and they'll never eat it. It's going to be one of those too. Okay, and both of those were great because breakfast is easy to do with shakes and bars, same thing for lunch. And with both of those you can sell very significant cost savings. Most people eat dinner at home so the cost savings is not as significant but don't let that deter you because then you have time savings when you're selling against dinner. So each of them have different benefits that you can sell against. Maybe I should have made a matrix for you but basically what is that? Breakfast and lunch is going to be easy and it's going to save them money. Dinner they're going to be saving time and prep and clean up and all that stuff. Okay, so what meal do you struggle the most with? They're going to tell you. You're like, awesome. What most people do is just replace the meals to struggle the most with safe time and money then you only need to think about breakfast and dinner. So that's in the instance of lunch. So what most people do is just replace the lunch and save some time and money and just only need to worry about breakfast and dinner. Sound good? Say I'm saying like, want to do that? Sound good? Besides it's nice to get some cool cooking ideas. Just from that it's just nice to get a couple cool cooking ideas from it and this way you won't have to think about anything with the shakes and bars. Great. So what do you prefer for that? Do you prefer chocolate or vanilla? Do you prefer X or Y? So you're going to do an assumed close for both the shakes and the bars and I want to be clear here. The reason that we sell shakes and bars separately is because if you notice on the back of a shake or a bar it says nutrition facts. On the back of supplements it says supplement facts and so one of them is food and the other one is supplements. Despite the fact that for some reason our world has lopped in shakes and bars as supplements they are not their food. I really genuinely believe that and if you think about it that way you will be able to sell twice as much product. If you try and lop it in you're only going to be accessing one purchase category which is going to be supplements. One wallet of that person. So you've already emptied the service wallet, emptied the supplements wallet and now we're going after the food wallet which is what they buy their groceries with, what they go out to eat with, all that which is another budget. If we can take some from there then we actually maximize the lifetime value of the customer. So I want to be really clear here you are not combining the supplement and the food sale they are separate. You've completed the supplement sale and then at that point is when you make the offer for replacing and saving the money. For example if they are spending 8 bucks a day on lunch or 12 because they eat out once a week and that's 30 bucks and the other 4 days they eat whatever just like that you are going to be saving them $150 a month by replacing their lunch with a shake. So you need to have that conviction because it's true and it's literally like it's going to be what they need and it's going to save them time and money. So it's a no brainer. So that completes that and then at the very end you are going to take their picture. So once you take their picture the weigh in process is complete. So I just want to be clear about that and make sure that when you have finished they know exactly when they are coming back, when their first work out is going to be and they understand how to use the app to schedule your work outs. If you have a scheduling system that's like that, if you do not have a scheduling system that's like that make sure that they understand when they are working out according to that process. Okay so I'm going to, we switch this to the last part of this process so you can spend as long as necessary closing the supplement and food sale and no one would slip out because everyone is still waiting to take the picture. So we just found that this was a great hack to get that done. We used to put the picture in the beginning and I put it at the end. People close more because people are just waiting around. I should have, I just want to make sure that everyone is clear. When I have the app portion in the beginning that also kind of includes the scheduling piece for them. So you want to make sure that they know when they walk out that door who is going to be reaching out to them, when their next workout is, what's expected, all that kind of jazz and that that has been explained to them at the onset. Alright lots of love, catch you soon and I'll see you on the other side.
